strengths: 20+ years of continuous software engineering experience across web, mobile, embedded, and infrastructure domains — exceeds the 15-year minimum requirement.
Active contributor to AI-agent infrastructure and MCP (Model Context Protocol) tooling at re:cinq, showing hands-on engagement with current agentic AI paradigms.

Demonstrated entrepreneurial track record: founded GISCollective, shipped multiple open-source frameworks, and built a top-10 Apple TV app — aligns with the preferred 'builder within larger orgs' profile.
concerns: No advanced degree (Master's or PhD) in AI or a related field; educational background is a BS in Computer Science and a fine arts degree, both below the preferred qualification bar for this seniority.

AI involvement is limited to the most recent role (April 2025–present) and scoped to developer tooling rather than strategic AI product ownership, platform architecture, or customer-facing AI advisory.

No evidence of director-level scope: the resume reflects consistent individual-contributor work with no documented ownership of cross-org technical strategy, executive stakeholder engagement, or large-scale AI platform leadership.

key_factors.factor: Breadth and longevity of engineering experience

response.justification: The candidate brings genuine technical depth and a long, varied engineering career that meets the experience floor, along with recent exposure to agentic AI tooling and a credible open-source/entrepreneurial track record. However, the role demands recognized AI expertise, director-level strategic influence, and the ability to advise enterprise customers on cloud and AI adoption — none of which are substantiated in the resume. AI work is both recent and scoped to infrastructure tooling rather than product strategy or platform leadership, and there is no indication of the external thought-leadership presence or executive-level stakeholder engagement the position requires.
